title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true
input_row.description: Enable client authentication on etcd service.
input_row.rationale: etcd is a highly-available key value store used by Kubernetes deployments for persistent storage of all of its REST API objects. These objects are sensitive in nature and should not be available to unauthenticated clients. You should enable the client authentication via valid certificates to secure the access to the etcd service.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the etcd server node: ps -ef | grep etcd Verify that the --client-cert-auth argument is set to true .
input_row.remediation: Edit the etcd pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/etcd.yaml on the master node and set the below parameter. --client-cert-auth="true"
input_row.impact: All clients attempting to access the etcd server will require a valid client certificate.
input_row.default_value: By default, the etcd service can be queried by unauthenticated clients.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_etcd_client_cert_auth_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the etcd pod configuration.
- The command `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o yaml | grep -i client-cert-auth` can be used to verify the presence of the `--client-cert-auth=true` argument in the etcd pod specification.
- Specifically, look for the command line arguments under the spec section of the etcd pod manifest.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The output includes `--client-cert-auth=true`.
  - **Fail:** The output does not include `--client-cert-auth=true` or the argument is missing entirely.
- Example: Execute `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -o jsonpath='{.items[?(@.metadata.name=="etcd-<node-name>")].spec.containers[*].command}'` to retrieve the command arguments for the etcd pod and check for the `--client-cert-auth=true` setting.
